app.name: Kinevo
app.tagline: Manage your personal training business with AI
app.description: Kinevo is a B2B SaaS platform for personal trainers. This connector lets trainers manage students, create training programs, prescribe exercises, track progress, send messages, and view financial data — all through natural language in ChatGPT.
app.long_description:
Kinevo connects personal trainers to their entire business operations directly from ChatGPT.

With 27 tools available, trainers can:

- Manage students — list, search, view profiles, create new students, update info and clinical conditions
- Build & edit training programs — create programs from scratch, add/rename/reschedule workout sessions, prescribe exercises with sets/reps/rest, edit or remove exercises in-place, and assign or expire programs
- Prescribe advanced methods — per-set schemes (pyramid, drop-set, cluster, 5x5, top+backoff) with per-set loads (kg or %1RM), and supersets/bi-sets/tri-sets
- Track progress — view workout history, load progression over time, adherence rates, form/check-in responses
- Communicate — list conversations, read messages, send messages to students (with push notifications)
- Monitor finances — view subscriptions, MRR, cancellations, payment status
- Get insights — dashboard summary with active students, programs, sessions, unread messages

Designed for the Brazilian personal training market. All tools support Portuguese prompts.

kinevo_ping.description: Test the connection to Kinevo. Returns trainer name and account status.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_list_students.description: List the trainer's students with optional filters. Returns active students by default. Use this to find students by name, check who is active/inactive, or get an overview of all students.

search.description: Filter by student name (partial match, case-insensitive)

status.description: Filter by student status. Defaults to all statuses if omitted.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_get_student.description: Get the complete profile of a specific student including personal data, clinical conditions, training history, and current program. Use this when the trainer asks about a specific student's details.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_create_student.description: Register a new student for this trainer. Creates the student profile with Supabase Auth account and optionally sets clinical/training preferences.

medical_restrictions.description: Medical conditions or restrictions (e.g., knee injury, herniated disc)

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool creates a new student account (auth user + profile record) in the trainer's Kinevo account.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new record. Does not modify or delete existing data. Reversible by the trainer via the dashboard.
justifications.open_world_justification: Creates a Supabase Auth user and student record within the trainer's own Kinevo account. No external services are contacted beyond Supabase Auth.

kinevo_update_student.description: Update an existing student's profile data such as name, phone, objective, modality, notes, or training level.

trainer_notes.description: Trainer's private notes about this student

status.description: Set student as active or inactive

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool updates an existing student's profile fields (name, phone, objective, notes, status).
justifications.destructive_justification: Modifies existing student data. Changes are reversible by updating again. No records are deleted.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only modifies data within the trainer's own Kinevo account. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_list_programs.description: List training programs created by this trainer. Can filter by student (to see their program history) or by status. Returns both program templates and assigned programs.

student_id.description: Filter programs assigned to a specific student

type.description: 'template' for reusable program templates, 'assigned' for programs assigned to students

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_get_program.description: Get full details of a training program including all workout sessions and exercises with sets, reps, and loads. Works for both templates and assigned programs.

type.description: Whether this is a template or an assigned program

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_create_program.description: Create a new training program template. This creates an empty program structure that can then have workout sessions and exercises added to it. Optionally assign it directly to a student.

student_id.description: If provided, creates the program as already assigned to this student (status: 'draft')

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool creates a new training program (as template or draft assigned to a student).
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new record. Does not modify or delete existing data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only creates data within the trainer's own Kinevo account.

kinevo_assign_program.description: Assign an existing program template to a student, creating a copy. Or activate a draft assigned program by setting its start date.

program_id.description: The program template ID (to copy) or assigned program ID (to activate)

student_id.description: Required when assigning a template. The student to assign to.

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today.

action.description: 'assign_template' copies a template to a student, 'activate_draft' activates an existing draft program

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool copies a program template to a student or activates a draft program.
justifications.destructive_justification: Copies data and changes program status. The original template is preserved. Previous active programs are deactivated (not deleted).
justifications.open_world_justification: Only modifies data within the trainer's own Kinevo account.

kinevo_expire_program.description: Manually expire/deactivate an active program. The program's data is preserved but it will no longer appear as the student's current program.

program_id.description: The assigned program ID to expire

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool changes an active program's status to expired.
justifications.destructive_justification: Changes status only. All program data (workouts, exercises) is preserved. No records are deleted.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only modifies data within the trainer's own Kinevo account.

kinevo_list_exercises.description: Search the exercise catalog. Returns exercises available to this trainer (system exercises + trainer's custom exercises). Filter by muscle group, equipment, or name.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_add_workout_session.description: Add a new workout session (e.g., 'Treino A - Peito e Triceps') to an existing program. Works for both templates and assigned programs.

program_id.description: The program ID to add the session to

program_type.description: Whether the program is a template or assigned

name.description: Session name (e.g., 'Treino A - Peito e Triceps')

order_index.description: Position in the program. If omitted, appends at the end.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool adds a new workout session to an existing training program.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new record within an existing program. Does not modify or delete existing sessions.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only creates data within the trainer's own Kinevo account.

kinevo_add_exercise_to_session.description: Add an exercise to a workout session. Supports both simple prescription (sets + reps + rest) and advanced per-set prescription (set_scheme) with different reps/load/rest per set, training methods (pyramid, drop-set, cluster, 5x5, top+backoff) and per-set load targets (kg or %1RM). Call kinevo_list_training_methods to see method presets. To group exercises into a superset, use kinevo_create_superset instead.

workout_id.description: The workout session ID to add the exercise to

exercise_id.description: The exercise ID from the catalog

sets.description: Number of sets (simple mode). Ignored when set_scheme is provided.

reps.description: Reps prescription (simple mode, e.g., '12', '8-12', 'AMRAP'). Ignored when set_scheme is provided.

rest_seconds.description: Rest between sets in seconds (simple mode)

exercise_function.description: The role of this exercise in the session

order_index.description: Position in the session. If omitted, appends at the end.

method_key.description: Training method label. Use with set_scheme. 'custom' for a hand-built scheme that matches no preset.

set_scheme.description: Advanced per-set prescription. Each entry is one set (or one phase of a round for compound methods). When provided, sets/reps/rest_seconds are derived from it. For drop-set/cluster pass ONE round here and set rounds>1.

reps.description: Reps for this set (e.g., '8', '8-12', 'AMRAP', '8+4+2' for cluster)

rest_seconds.description: Rest in seconds AFTER this set

weight_target_kg.description: Suggested absolute load in kg for this set

weight_target_pct1rm.description: Suggested load as % of 1RM for this set

rounds.description: Number of rounds the set_scheme repeats (compound methods like drop-set/cluster). Defaults to 1 (linear methods).

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool adds an exercise (simple or advanced per-set scheme) to an existing workout session.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new record within an existing workout. Does not modify or delete existing exercises.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only creates data within the trainer's own Kinevo account.

kinevo_update_workout_session.description: Rename, reorder, or reschedule an existing workout session in a program. Use this to edit a session in-place without recreating the program — including setting which weekdays the session falls on (scheduled_days).

workout_id.description: The workout session ID to update

scheduled_days.description: Days of the week this session is scheduled on (0=Sunday … 6=Saturday). E.g. [1,3,5] = Mon/Wed/Fri. Pass [] to clear the schedule.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool updates an existing workout session's name, position, or weekly schedule.
justifications.destructive_justification: Modifies existing session fields only. No exercises or sessions are deleted. Changes are reversible by updating again.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only modifies data within the trainer's own Kinevo account.

kinevo_delete_workout_session.description: Delete a workout session from a program. This permanently removes the session and all its exercises (cascade). Use to clean up old sessions when editing a program in-place.

workout_id.description: The workout session ID to delete

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool deletes a workout session and its exercises from a program.
justifications.destructive_justification: Permanently removes a workout session and its child exercises (cascade) from the trainer's own program. The trainer initiates this explicitly to clean up a session. Other sessions and student workout history (logged sessions) are not affected.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only deletes data within the trainer's own Kinevo account. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_update_workout_item.description: Edit an exercise inside a workout session in-place: change sets, reps, rest, notes, function, training method, position, or swap the exercise itself. Also supports advanced per-set prescription via set_scheme (replaces the whole per-set scheme). Only the fields you pass are changed.

item_id.description: The workout item (exercise) ID to update

exercise_id.description: Swap to a different exercise from the catalog

sets.description: Number of sets (simple mode). Ignored when set_scheme is provided.

reps.description: Reps prescription (simple mode). Ignored when set_scheme is provided.

rest_seconds.description: Rest between sets in seconds (simple mode)

notes.description: Special instructions. Pass null to clear.

exercise_function.description: The role of this exercise in the session

method_key.description: Training method label. Pass null or 'standard' to clear.

set_scheme.description: Advanced per-set prescription. Replaces the entire existing scheme. Pass an empty array [] to remove the scheme and revert to simple mode. When non-empty, sets/reps/rest_seconds are derived from it.

rounds.description: Rounds the set_scheme repeats (compound methods). Defaults to 1.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool edits an existing exercise inside a workout session (sets, reps, rest, method, or swaps the exercise).
justifications.destructive_justification: Modifies an existing workout item in place. Replacing a set_scheme rewrites that item's per-set rows, but no other records are deleted and changes are reversible by updating again.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only modifies data within the trainer's own Kinevo account.

kinevo_create_superset.description: Create a superset (or bi-set/tri-set) inside a workout session: a group of exercises performed back-to-back with rest taken only after the group. Creates the superset container plus its child exercises in one call. Works for templates and assigned programs.

workout_id.description: The workout session ID to add the superset to

rest_seconds.description: Rest in seconds after each round of the superset

order_index.description: Position of the superset in the session. If omitted, appends at the end.

exercises.description: The exercises in the superset, in order. At least 2.

sets.description: Number of rounds/sets for this exercise

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool creates a superset container and its child exercises inside an existing workout session.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates new records within an existing workout. Does not modify or delete existing exercises.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only creates data within the trainer's own Kinevo account.

kinevo_delete_workout_item.description: Remove an exercise from a workout session. This permanently deletes the item (and any superset children attached to it). Use to clean up exercises when editing a session in-place.

item_id.description: The workout item (exercise) ID to delete

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool deletes a single exercise (and any superset children) from a workout session.
justifications.destructive_justification: Permanently removes one workout item from the trainer's own program. The trainer initiates this explicitly to clean up an exercise. No other items or student workout history are affected.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only deletes data within the trainer's own Kinevo account. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_list_training_methods.description: List the advanced prescription capabilities available when building or editing workouts: training-method presets (pyramid, drop-set, cluster, 5x5, top+backoff), the per-set scheme fields, the valid set types, and how supersets work. Call this before prescribing advanced methods so you pass the right method_key, set_scheme, and rounds to kinevo_add_exercise_to_session / kinevo_update_workout_item.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads the catalog of training-method presets (system presets plus the trainer's own saved presets). No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_get_student_progress.description: Get a student's training progress including workout history, adherence rate, and load progression over time. Can filter by date range and specific exercise for load tracking.

days.description: Number of days to look back

exercise_id.description: If provided, returns load progression for this specific exercise

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_get_form_responses.description: Get form/check-in responses submitted by a student. Includes pre/post-workout check-ins, anamneses, and surveys.

trigger_context.description: Filter by how the form was triggered

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_get_dashboard_summary.description: Get an overview of the trainer's account: total students, active programs, students without programs, students inactive for 7+ days, recent activity, and key metrics.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_send_message.description: Send a text message to a student. The message appears in the student's Kinevo app inbox.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool sends a text message to a student, creating a message record and triggering a push notification.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new message record. Does not modify or delete existing messages. The student receives a push notification on their device.
justifications.open_world_justification: Sends a push notification to the student's registered device via Expo Push API. The message content is visible to the student in their Kinevo mobile app.

kinevo_list_subscriptions.description: List student contracts/subscriptions managed by this trainer. Shows payment status, plan, amount, and next billing date.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_get_revenue_summary.description: Get a financial summary: MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue), new contracts this month, cancellations, and payment status overview.

month.description: Month in YYYY-MM format. Defaults to current month.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_list_conversations.description: List the trainer's conversations with students, ordered by most recent message. Shows unread count for each conversation.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

kinevo_get_conversation.description: Get messages from a conversation with a specific student, ordered by most recent first.

before.description: Fetch messages before this timestamp (for pagination)

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool only reads data from the trainer's account. No records are created, modified, or deleted.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not modify or delete any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool only accesses the trainer's own Kinevo account data, scoped by their authenticated session. No external systems are contacted.

test_account.notes: Test trainer account with 3 sample students (Maria Silva, Carlos Santos, Ana Oliveira). Subscription: trialing (90 days). After logging in on the browser, connect the MCP server — the OAuth flow will use this session.

testing.instructions:
1. Log in at https://www.kinevoapp.com/login with the test credentials above
2. Connect to the MCP server at https://www.kinevoapp.com/api/mcp using OAuth
3. The OAuth consent screen will show OpenAI Reviewer — click Autorizar
4. Test read tools: List my active students, Show my dashboard summary
5. Test write tools: Send a message to Maria Silva: Hello from ChatGPT!
6. Test complex flow: Create a 4-week hypertrophy program for Carlos Santos

Note: Brazilian SaaS for personal trainers. Prompts work in Portuguese and English.
test_prompts: Me da um resumo geral da minha conta
Quais exercicios de peitoral tem no catalogo?
Quais alunos nao treinaram nos ultimos 7 dias?
